problem:
The United States Postal Service charges an extra $\$0.11$ in postage if the length of an envelope, in inches, divided by its height, in inches, is less than $1.3$ or greater than $2.5.$ For how many of these four envelopes must the extra $\$0.11$ in postage be paid? \begin{tabular}[t]{ccc}
Envelope & Length in inches & Height in inches\\\hline
A &6 &4\\
B &9 &3\\
C &6 &6\\
D &11 &4
\end{tabular}

classified_questions.question: What is the requirement for an envelope to be charged an extra $\$0.11$ in postage by the United States Postal Service?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Can you calculate the ratio of length to height for each envelope in this problem?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Which envelopes have a ratio that is less than $1.3$ or greater than $2.5$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many envelopes must pay the extra $\$0.11$ in postage according to the given requirements?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Is it possible for an envelope to have a ratio that is exactly $1.3$ or $2.5$? If so, would it be charged the extra $\$0.11$ in postage?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: If an envelope is charged the extra $\$0.11$ in postage, can we determine its ratio of length to height?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How would the answer change if the requirement for the extra $\$0.11$ in postage was different? For example, what if the requirement was a ratio less than $1.2$ or greater than $2.8$?
classified_questions.label: b
